items.summary: Visa and Mastercard are actively racing to set agentic commerce standards — Mastercard co-developed a new framework with Adyen providing 'cryptographic proof of what a consumer actually authorized' for AI-agent transactions. This is a direct build into the exact upstream intent-verification layer Revaly's RTN occupies. If the card networks own the intent-signal standard, it changes Revaly's RTN architecture story — accelerates the need for Capital One/BoA/Velera rails integrations before Visa/MC commoditize the space.
items.source: Payments Dive — Visa/Mastercard Agentic Standards, March 2026

items.summary: Mastercard's Recorded Future Annual Payment Fraud Report 2026 warns fraud is growing in scale and sophistication, and organizations that succeed will 'fuse cyber and fraud intelligence and maximize signal detection across fragmented data sources.' Simultaneously, PYMNTS reports merchants are fighting back as fraud gets cheaper to execute. The fraud intelligence signal layer is becoming a boardroom priority — Revaly's RTN data rails are positioned at exactly this intersection of approval optimization and fraud intelligence.

JPMorgan's latest fintech infrastructure analysis (covered by CrowdfundInsider, March 23) identifies six critical infrastructure areas payment leaders must invest in as real-time payments gain momentum — forcing fintech executives to make tough capital allocation decisions. This is directly relevant to Revaly's Q3 2026 growth equity pitch: as payments infrastructure investment cycles accelerate, RTN/bi-directional data sharing rails become a credible institutional investment thesis.

Chargebacks911 published updated 2026 chargeback stats: merchants using automated dispute responses recorded a 33% reduction in chargeback cases. Merchants win disputes only 20-30% of the time and recover revenue just 18% of the time. These metrics underscore the upstream prevention opportunity Revaly's RTN rails address — stopping chargebacks before they happen vs. fighting them after. Chargebacks911 is entrenching in the reactive layer while Revaly can own the proactive layer.

FirstCreditOnline published 'Why Failed Payment Recovery Needs a Digital-First Strategy in 2026' — citing AI personalization, self-service options, and compliance-built automation as the new baseline. The piece explicitly calls out that basic retry logic is table stakes and recovery now requires AI-driven engagement flows. This validates Revaly's Engaged Recovery product positioning and provides fresh buyer language. Article published one week ago and already surfacing in search for core recovery queries.

Two more 2026 billing platform roundups (ThriveCart and ConnectPay, both published in the past 2 weeks) continue featuring Recurly's ML-powered dunning as a category differentiator — with ConnectPay specifically noting Recurly's ML 'turns what would have been lost revenue into retained income.' The bundled-recovery narrative is gaining SEO momentum. No new Recurly product announcements, but the sustained content flywheel is actively shaping buyer perception of standalone recovery as redundant.

Kount (Equifax) continues to appear in 2026 fraud prevention comparisons as a leading tool for eCommerce and subscription chargeback prevention. No new product announcements or funding activity detected this week. Kount's Equifax data infrastructure remains a medium-term watch — if they extend from fraud prevention into approval optimization using credit bureau data, they'd be approaching Revaly's RTN thesis from the issuer data side.

Vindicia continues to appear in the GlobeNewswire Subscription Billing Management Market Report (published March 13, still generating coverage) alongside Chargebee, Recurly, and Stripe as a named player in the $X hybrid subscription market. No Vindicia-specific product launches, acquisitions, or partnerships detected this week. Their presence remains analyst-report driven. The hybrid subscription + usage-based shift cited in the report does expand Revaly's TAM.

Stripe and Paradigm launched the Machine Payments Protocol (MPP) on March 18 — an open standard enabling AI agents to pay for services autonomously. Visa, Mastercard, Anthropic, OpenAI, and Shopify have already integrated it. This is a significant structural shift: if agentic commerce bypasses human-initiated payment flows, Revaly's upstream RTN intent-capture and approval optimization rails become directly load-bearing infrastructure for a new commerce layer. Simultaneously, a Citrini Research scenario circulating in Feb 2026 warns AI agents will identify Visa/MC's 2-3% interchange as a cost to eliminate via stablecoins — a potential rails disruption to monitor.

Chargebacks911 CTO Donald Kossmann published commentary on agentic commerce security risks — warning that AI agents making purchases introduce a new dispute category where 'I didn't buy that, my AI did' becomes a chargeback trigger. Simultaneously, Chargebacks911's own stats show fraud costs hitting $4.61 per dollar lost in 2025 (up 37% vs 2020), with 2026 fraud losses expected to set records. Chargebacks911 is running the highest PR velocity of any competitor this week across multiple media. Their upstream move via the Monavate programme-manager channel plus this agentic fraud narrative positions them as an RTN-adjacent threat at the issuer layer.

FirstCreditOnline published 'Why Failed Payment Recovery Needs a Digital-First Strategy in 2026' — emphasizing AI personalization, self-service options, and compliance-built automation as the new table stakes for dunning. Separately, a Startupik piece on CFO priorities confirms that in 2026 'pricing is changing faster, CFOs want cleaner revenue data, and teams suddenly care about failed payments, churn, and usage billing far more than they did a year ago.' Both pieces validate Revaly's market timing and provide fresh buyer-language for sales and marketing.

Chargebacks911 CEO Monica Eaton is running two sustained PR narratives this week: (1) 'I didn't buy that, my AI did' — agentic commerce removes the click as intent signal, creating a new chargeback wave; (2) friendly fraud is now an 'epidemic' per Sky News coverage. Chargebacks911 has the highest PR velocity in the category — 3+ press hits in the last week spanning Finextra, Sky News, and Business Money. Revaly's RTN intent-capture at the issuer level is architecturally positioned to solve the exact upstream problem Eaton is publicly defining.
items.source: Finextra / Sky News / Business Money — March 2026

items.summary: FinTech Weekly published 'Payments Innovation Theatre Is Failing Merchants' — arguing that complexity-adding innovation without improving approval rates or revenue is endemic. Author calls for infrastructure-level solutions focused on revenue and approvals. This is direct editorial validation of Revaly's core positioning: the market is actively critiquing point-in-time complexity tools and calling for what Revaly's RTN rails deliver. Useful as third-party positioning ammunition in sales and investor narratives.

A new technical piece on transaction routing platforms describes AI predicting processor outages before they happen and analyzing historical data to route each transaction to the acquirer most likely to approve it — called a 'self-healing payment ecosystem.' By 2026, this predictive approval routing is being framed as standard infrastructure. This validates Revaly's RTN/approval optimization architecture as the direction the industry is heading, but also signals that general-purpose payment routing vendors are encroaching on the approval optimization narrative.
items.source: Bamboodt — Transaction Routing Fintech Platform, March 2026

items.summary: Fresh benchmark data from Finsi.ai shows payment failure rates of 12-16% for subscription box companies, 8-12% for B2C SaaS, and 5-8% for B2B SaaS — with involuntary churn accounting for 20-40% of total churn across segments. A separate industry source confirms average B2B SaaS involuntary churn at 0.8% monthly. These benchmarks provide concrete TAM validation for Revaly's recovery and RTN products. Useful data to anchor ROI conversations with prospects.

Multiple 2026 SaaS billing platform roundups continue to feature Recurly's ML-powered dunning as a top differentiator — with new coverage from ConnectPay and TechBullion this week. The bundled-recovery narrative ('your billing platform handles this') is gaining SEO momentum as a buyer objection. Revaly needs sharper differentiation proof points on recovery delta vs. Recurly's native dunning to counter this in mid-market sales conversations.

Chargebacks911 × Monavate plug-and-play dispute management partnership confirmed live as of March 17 — still generating press 3 days later. Chargebacks911 is now embedded directly into a card programme manager's stack, meaning their dispute automation runs at the issuer/programme layer, not just the merchant layer. This upstream channel move directly overlaps Revaly's RTN connectivity story. Brad Wiskerchen's Chargebacks911 relationship makes a strategic dialogue timely — whether competitive intelligence, partnership, or positioning alignment.

Tuhk continues to rank prominently in Canadian fintech 2026 watchlists as building 'a collaborative payments platform enabling secure, real-time data sharing between merchants, banks, and service providers to combat global fraud.' This is structurally identical to Revaly's RTN bi-directional rails thesis. No new funding or product announcements surfaced this week — but their sustained PR presence in the same fintech media channels Revaly operates in indicates active BD and fundraising posture.
items.source: Fintech.ca — Canadian Fintech Startups to Watch 2026

items.summary: The Nacha ACH compliance mandate took effect March 20, 2026. Subscription merchants reviewing their payments stack are at peak receptivity for RTN/approval optimization conversations. The second punch (Visa subscription mandate) lands April 18. The two-week window between mandates is the highest-leverage GTM moment of Q1 for outreach to merchants in active compliance review mode.

Mastercard published 'Verifiable Intent' as a formal framework for proving user authorization in agentic AI transactions. Mastercard is building its own solution for the same upstream signal layer Revaly's RTN rails occupy. This validates the RTN thesis but signals that Visa/MC networks are beginning to develop proprietary intent-verification infrastructure that could commoditize the signal layer over time.
items.source: Mastercard.com — Verifiable Intent for Agentic AI Commerce, March 2026
